items.title: Like Bristol-Myers Squibb And AbbVie, Johnson & Johnson Seeks To Bolster Neuropsychiatric Portfolio With $15 Billion Deal
items.author: Vandana Singh
items.description: Johnson & Johnson agreed to acquire Intra-Cellular Therapies for $14.6 billion, adding the company's central nervous system-focused therapies, including the approved drug Caplyta, to its portfolio.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Intra-Cellular Therapies is being acquired by Johnson & Johnson for a significant premium, indicating the value of its central nervous system-focused therapies.
Johnson & Johnson is expanding its portfolio of central nervous system therapies through the acquisition of Intra-Cellular Therapies, which could strengthen its position in this market.
The article mentions a previous acquisition by Bristol-Myers Squibb, but this is not the focus of the current news.

items.title: 2 Beaten-Down Dividend Stocks to Buy in 2025
items.author: Prosper Junior Bakiny
items.description: CVS Health and Johnson & Johnson are attractive dividend stocks despite facing some headwinds. CVS Health's long-term strategy and strong dividend program make it a solid income stock, while Johnson & Johnson's stability, adaptability, and secure dividend make it an excellent pick for income-seeking investors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: CVS Health's long-term strategy, including its ecosystem of healthcare services, and its strong dividend program make it an attractive option for income-seeking investors, despite its current financial headwinds.

Johnson & Johnson's stability, adaptability, and secure dividend program with a 62-year streak of consecutive increases make it an excellent pick for income-seeking investors, despite facing some legal and regulatory challenges.

items.title: Ophthalmic Drugs Global Market Report 2025: Innovations in Retinal Disorders, Glaucoma Drive Global Ophthalmic Medicines Surge
items.author: Researchandmarkets.Com
items.description: The global ophthalmic drugs market is expected to surpass $35 billion in 2025, driven by rising incidences of eye-related conditions and a strong pipeline of new drugs. However, side effects of topical ophthalmic drugs may limit market growth, necessitating a comprehensive strategy to address safety concerns.
